title: Melkor benchmark manifest v1
description: Declares a reproducible benchmark: what is measured, on what data, against what comparator, and how. A manifest is the input; a benchmark-result is the output. No number appears in a public Melkor surface without a result produced from a manifest like this.

id.description: Stable, versioned identifier, e.g. 'format-roundtrip-v1'. A claim links to this.

category.description: Which kind of thing this measures. Kept separate on purpose: a format-fidelity number is not a pipeline-quality number, and mixing them is how misleading comparisons happen.

items.description: A dataset ID resolved through datasets/manifest.json. The data itself is never in git.

comparators.description: Independent tools compared against, each pinned by version. A comparison to an unpinned tool is not reproducible.

attribution_only.description: True when the comparator's own reported numbers are cited but NOT reproduced by Melkor. Such numbers must never be combined into a Melkor-measured table.

implementation.description: The exact metric implementation and version. PSNR/SSIM/LPIPS differ between libraries; the implementation is part of the number.

repetitions.description: How many times each measurement is repeated. A single run has no reportable variance.

seed.description: Fixed RNG seed; a wall-clock seed is not reproducible.

limits_profile.description: The resource-limit profile the operation runs under, because limits affect which inputs are even accepted.

expiry.description: When this manifest's results should be considered stale and re-measured.

invalidated_by.description: Changes that expire the claim immediately, e.g. 'spz-upstream-revision', 'khr-gaussian-splatting-revision'.
